page_content: AAST421 Asian American Public Policy (3 Credits) Using Asian Paciﬁc Americans as a case study, this course will analyze the development of public policy in America. Each week, topics such as community development, voting rights, and the movement to redress the wartime internment of Japanese Americans will serve as backdrops for discussion. We will explore the policy-making roles of legislators, judges, local and national political leaders, journalists, writers, unions, social movements, and community organizations. Cross-listed with: AMST418N. Credit Only Granted for: AAST421, AAST498M or AMST418N. Formerly: AAST498M.
